core_thesis: Vivekananda restates Advaita as a practical metaphysic: the ātman is non-different from Brahman, and this identity obliges the re-reading of work, worship, and service as modes of realizing the same truth in life. Jñāna therefore does not abolish the world into insignificance; it grounds a Practical Vedānta in which fearlessness, strength, and service follow from seeing the one Self in all beings ([Complete Works I, Jnana-Yoga](cite://vivekananda/complete-works/1.jnana-yoga); [Complete Works II, Practical Vedanta](cite://vivekananda/complete-works/2.practical-vedanta)).
